meaning: adj.被弃的,孤独的,被抛弃的
examples: He doubted their claim to have forsaken military solutions to the civil war.
The delta region of the Rio Grande river was a forsaken land of thickets and swamps.
His sister wept, and begged him not to go, "If they kill you, dear fawn, I shall be here alone in the forest, forsaken by the whole world."
These were hard-nosed men who would have forsaken their spleens for a couple of victories.
But at Cannes euro-zone leaders made plain that family members could be forsaken, even disinherited.
